title: Crispy Tangy Ukrainian Chicken Bowl
description: A highprotein, postworkout bowl featuring sousvide chicken, crunchy shallots, bright lemonpaprika sauce, and nutty buckwheat, all topped with a dollop of sour cream and fresh dill.

ingredients.name: buckwheat berries
ingredients.notes: rinse and cook according to package

instructions: Season the chicken pieces with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, minced garlic, and half of the lemon zest.
Place seasoned chicken in a vacuum‑seal bag or a zip‑lock bag and add a tablespoon of olive oil. Seal the bag using the water‑displacement method.
Set your sous‑vide immersion circulator to 145 °F (63 °C) and cook the chicken for 1 hour 30 minutes.
While the chicken cooks, rinse the buckwheat berries and cook them according to package instructions; usually 15 minutes in salted water, then drain.

After the sous‑vide time, remove chicken from the bag and pat dry with paper towels. Heat a large skillet over high heat, add a splash of oil, and sear the chicken until a crisp golden crust forms, about 2 minutes per side.
Meanwhile, in a small pan, sauté the sliced shallots in a teaspoon of butter until caramelized, about 5 minutes. Stir in the remaining lemon zest and juice to finish the sauce.
To assemble the bowl, place a scoop of cooked buckwheat at the bottom, top with the crispy chicken, and drizzle the shallot‑lemon sauce over the top.
Finish with a dollop of sour cream and a sprinkle of fresh dill.
Serve hot and enjoy the burst of tangy, crunchy flavors.
